scope: TorchTitan DeepSeek-V4 sparse-MLA/LightningIndexer manual_recompute backward bridge validation at S512.

mode.intent: Save original Q/K/V, compressed K/V, sparse indices, and masks, then recompute branch-local selected K/V and softmax probabilities during backward instead of saving selected K/V/probs from forward.
still_materializes_transiently: the same tensors again during backward recompute
mode.not_final: This mode reduces saved activation state but still materializes selected state transiently. The final S4096 path still needs a fused sparse MLA/LightningIndexer kernel that streams selected K/V and probabilities without building the large tensors.

check_config.command: env CANARY_SPARSE_MLA_BACKWARD_MODE=manual_recompute CANARY_SEQ_LEN=512 CANARY_GBS=64 CANARY_STEPS=1 GPUS_PER_NODE=4 launchers/torchtitan_direct_deepseek_v4_4x8xmi350_canary.sh check-config

remote_container_parity.check: Compared _SparseMLARecomputeManualBackward against _SparseMLASplitManualBackward on a mixed-validity CPU sparse pattern.

comparison.read: Recomputing selected K/V and probabilities in backward is correct but did not reduce the observed S512 4x peak memory; it also slightly reduced throughput. This points at forward-time selected-state/prob materialization as the next barrier, not just saved backward activations.

next: Do not spend more time on saved-state-only reshuffles.
Move to a forward+backward fused sparse MLA/LightningIndexer path that avoids transient selected K/V and probability tensors.
Use manual_recompute only as a diagnostic control for activation-save attribution.
